title: The history of Palazzo Pitti and the Medici family
highlights: of Palazzo Pitti is fundamentally tied to the royal dynasties. Over the course of many centuries, it has in fact hosted three noble families: the Medici, the Lorena and finally the Savoy. The name of the palazzo derives from the Pitti family, precisely from Luca Pitti, who in the 15th century was a great rival of the hated Medicis. History tells us that the Medici family, led by Cosimo the Elder (il Vecchio), wanted to  ...    ruin hit the family. The same thing would happen in the following years to the Strozzi family and Palazzo Strozzi. Nearly 100 years later, in 1550, Palazzo Pitti was acquired by Eleonora di Toledo, wife of Cosimo I de Medici and daughter of the viceroy of Naples. The duchess was looking for a healthier locale for herself and her children, away  ...    Under the Medic

# The Florence of Cosimo I ### All the places that tell of the first grand duke of Tuscany, who revolutionized the city This spring, 450 years will have passed since that 21 April 1574 when Cosimo I de’ Medici, the second Duke of Florence and first Grand Duke of Tuscany, died in Palazzo Pitti. Cosimo had the ambition to bring a project to life that would  ...    his childhood in the green hills of Mugello. His life took a turn in 1537, when upon the death of Alessandro, he obtained the Duchy of Tuscany.
